title: The Prairie
author: James Fenimore Cooper
content: “The earth was the heritage of man, and he was bound to contend for it.”
action: Stake your claim and defend your ground.

title: Narrative of the Life
author: Frederick Douglass
content: “I have found that, to make a contented slave, it is necessary to make a thoughtless one.”
action: Refuse to yield your autonomy to thoughtlessness.

title: The Last of the Mohicans
author: James Fenimore Cooper
content: “The path is narrow, and the enemy is near.”
action: Tighten your perimeter; remove the vulnerability.

title: Journal of the Discovery
author: William Clark
action: Move forward until you reach the objective.

title: Life on the Mississippi
author: Mark Twain
content: “I had to learn the river; not just the water, but the bottom.”
action: Master the terrain beneath your operation.
